jobs.title: GWM Finance Intern
jobs.description:
Are you interest in Finance? Do you have an eye for detail and have a strong analytical skillset?
We are looking for an intern to:

• prepare weekly and monthly standard reporting for head quarter
• develop analytical controls and integrated reconciliation spreadsheets and processes
• track banker performance at client and product level
• support end users on Business Intelligence tools, supporting on Dashboards preparation/correction and on the tool administrative management
• automate manual processes and assist in the technological development of the financial area
• introduce process improvements and implement financial best practices

The GWM Finance Intern is responsible for providing support with analysis and insight of WM results in coordination with the broader Finance team.
This role will give you the opportunity to build a strong knowledge base across multiple finance specialist areas including accounting, product control and tax planning.
You’ll also support on technical routines related to business intelligence tools as Qlik Sense and Alteryx.

jobs.title: Registered Client Associate, (Client Experience & Marketing)
jobs.description:
Deliver a best in class client experience. Elevate the team’s brand. Make the partners more effective. This role sits at the intersection of client service, marketing/brand execution, and partner leverage. You’ll ensure clients are well taken care of, the team is brand is delivered professionally, and partners stay focused on high value work.

What you’ll own:
• •manage client service workflows: account opening, maintenance, documentation, and follow through
• •keep CRM and management systems accurate, current, and reliable
• •prepare meeting materials, reports, and post meeting deliverables
• •elevate the Clear Point Partners brand through social media and digital channels (content coordination, posting support, consistency of voice and look)
• •maintain and refresh marketing materials; coordinate client events and campaigns
• •support partners with calendars, travel, expenses, and ad hoc administrative or personal tasks
• •proactively manage the team calendar so priorities, deadlines, and events don’t slip

jobs.title: Instrument reference Data Specialist
jobs.description:
Data Steward create, maintain & provide access to data. They define key data under their management and implement efficient and effective data management process as well as adequate controls to ensure that data is provided in good quality and in line with UBS policies and commercial agreements.
Key Responsibilities
• Design implement and execute processes to create and maintain data
• Apply a broad range of data management skills, from information management, non-financial risk control, data quality issue management, data cleaning, data governance to ensure that data is properly managed, shared and preserved
• Ensure the quality, security, reliability, and policy compliance of data
• Design, build, and test data quality controls and business rules, monitor data quality and manage rules exceptions.
• Remediate data quality issues
• Apply concepts and assume responsibilities as defined in the Data Management Framework
• Document and continuously improve efficiency and robustness of data management process.
• Manage day-to-day stakeholder interaction.

titleByLocale.it: Product Manager, Americas Securities settlements & Confirmations
titleByLocale.en: Product Manager, Americas Securities settlements & Confirmations
titleByLocale.de: Product Manager, Americas Securities settlements & Confirmations
titleByLocale.fr: Product Manager, Americas Securities settlements & Confirmations

jobs.description:
Do you have an entrepreneurial mindset and are you eager to understand and fulfill our clients’ needs? Do you want to play a key role in transforming our firm into an agile organization?

At UBS, we re-imagine the way we work, the way we connect with each other – our colleagues, clients and partners – and the way we deliver value. Being agile will make us more responsive, more adaptable, and ultimately more innovative.

We’re looking for a Product Manager to:

• co-develop a clear product vision, strategy and roadmap covering the full product development lifecycle (including creation, version and infrastructure upgrades, and eventual decommissioning)
• drive new products and product changes iteratively and continuously together with cross-functional team members in the pod
• act as product owner within an agile team, participating in agile ceremonies and ensuring the product requirements are clear to designers and engineers.
• ensure the product is being tested together with clients to gain insights and ensure readiness to launch before market entry (e.g., UX testing, end user testing and research insights)
• support pod Objective and Key Results and provide relevant product measurements and metrics to meet overall business goals
• ensure the quality and compliance of the product, from also a technical and non-functional requirement (i.e. production stability, performance, maintainability)

jobs.title: Scrum Master
jobs.description:
Do you have a knack for technology? Are you at your best when supporting others?
We are seeking a highly experienced Scrum Master & Agile Project Lead to join our technology team in a mission-critical financial environment. As a Scrum Master, you will lead Agile teams (Pods) through the delivery lifecycle, ensuring adherence to Agile principles and practices. This role is ideal for someone who has a proven track record as a servant leader, removing impediments, fostering collaboration, and driving continuous improvement in regulated industries such as banking or financial services. You will also support Agile transformation initiatives across the Stream.

jobs.title: IB Business Control Unit (BCU) Client Risk Manager

titleByLocale.it: IB Business Control Unit (BCU) Client Risk Manager
titleByLocale.en: IB Business Control Unit (BCU) Client Risk Manager
titleByLocale.de: IB Business Control Unit (BCU) Client Risk Manager
titleByLocale.fr: IB Business Control Unit (BCU) Client Risk Manager

jobs.description:
Do you have experience in operation of client risk identification and escalation processes, as well as management of associated documentation, ongoing process improvement, development of sophisticated presentation materials and various governance activities.

We are looking for a Business Risk Expert who will be working in the Business Control Unit (BCU) within the IB COO Business Risk Management Team who will be responsible for :

• •Participation in global client risk and reporting processes, including presentation, escalation and tracking to resolution of risk issues
• •Provide support for core BCU processes, including governance activities
• •Provide ongoing enhancement to the effectiveness and efficiency of BCU processes
• •Elements of regulatory and Internal Audit engagement with respect to the BCU program and as a contributor to various related inquiries and exams
• •Maintain client risk related documentation to a high standard, satisfactory to internal and external stakeholders
• •Proactive identification and execution of new opportunities for the BCU to positively impact client risk management culture and outcomes
• •Provide support to help drive ad hoc client escalations to resolution
• •Participation in strategic data initiatives of the BCU

jobs.title: Securities Specialist
jobs.description:
Are you detail oriented, even when multitasking? Do you know how to work with a team to achieve a common goal?

We’re looking for someone like that to help us:
- Collaborate with external clients and internal clients to improve processes, drive outcomes and build lasting relationships
– Process transactions, confirmations and manage exceptions and risk
– Perform root cause analysis to identify and drive process improvements
– Deliver timely and complete resolutions to the client
– Assist with new client set up and ensure the first trades are processed correctly
– Proactively manage the Client experience, anticipate needs and provide excellent client service
– Collaborate across Operations team to identify process efficiencies and solutions

Detailed salary information :
• Nashville: the salary range for this role is $50000 to $65000
The expected salary range(s) for this role as of the date of this posting is/are based on factors including, but not limited to, experience, qualifications, education, location and skill level. This role may also be eligible for discretionary incentive compensation. For benefits information, please visit ubs.com/usbenefits .

jobs.title: Reporting and Automation Analyst
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about data? Are you looking to drive key product and regulatory initiatives across banking products such as SBL, Mortgages, Cards and Deposits? We are looking for someone like that who will analyze product, Financial Advisor and client data, support product development, drive/support revenue-generating initiatives and work very closely with product management, control and support partners.
Key competencies and responsibilities include:

• Develop data-driven business insights and analytics to drive growth within Banking
• Key contributor to strategic initiatives and part of a team that works on business growth development initiatives
• Deliver insights and segmentation by leveraging various internal and external data to grow market share of banking products and services and support strategic banking decision making
• Use product/financial SME to demonstrate through reporting and analyses how the product area drives profitability and work to develop strategies to enhance revenues
• Develop and assist in various reporting initiatives and data analytics on banking initiatives
• Take an ownership role in product data capabilities and work within the team and with partners to ensure sound data governance and development frameworks
• Analyze complex business problems and issues using data from internal and external sources to provide insight to decision-makers
• Identify and interpret trends and patterns in datasets to construct recommendations for strategic/tactical sales plans

• assist in the management of a team and planning of overall resource requirements, by recruiting, developing and retaining talented individuals

jobs.title: Audit Expert - Financial Crime Prevention
jobs.description:
Are you a thoughtful evaluator of risk who enjoys understanding complex business processes and products? Do you want to play a role in how a global organization strengthens its controls and adapts in a fast moving environment?

As an Audit Expert in Group Internal Audit, you will:

• •plan and deliver high quality, risk based audits, applying sound judgement to generate meaningful insights
• •assess the design and effectiveness of internal controls, highlighting strong practices and where risks require management attention
• •use AI and data analytics to enhance audit work and develop data driven testing and insights
• •develop clear, evidence based audit findings and reports, escalating significant concerns appropriately
• •communicate complex topics in a concise and constructive way to stakeholders, helping promote risk awareness
• •collaborate effectively with peers across audit teams and build trusted relationships with stakeholders at all levels
• •coverage may include (for example): financial crime risks (e.g., AML, KYC, sanctions, anti bribery & corruption)

jobs.title: Americas Compliance Audit Manager
jobs.description:
Are you a sharp evaluator of risk? Can you investigate complex situations and propose solutions? We're looking for someone like that who can:
• lead or conduct reviews and audits of specific business areas, provide practical, innovative and value-added solutions to issues identified
• drive or work as a team member on reviews and audits, including thematic and front-to-back
• analyze and assess risks and barriers to delivery assumed by both the business and support functions
• identify and evaluate the effectiveness of controls designed to address those risks
• prepare reports of audit findings for UBS senior management, monitor the results, risk profile and developments across Financial Crime Prevention and provide input for planning sessions
• successfully collaborate with peers in and across audit teams
• Builds-up and continuously maintains sufficient knowledge of organizational and governance structure, of products and processes, of legal and regulatory requirements, as well as developing professional skills and experience, to allow for an effective and professional mandate execution.

jobs.description:

• act as a primary point of contact for clients, responding to their demands with agility and efficiency
• manage all operational and administrative account processes, including the organization and maintenance of client documentation and Know Your Client (KYC)
• coordinate closely with stakeholders to align client requests and ensure the delivery of exceptional service quality
• foster a risk-aware culture with the team by ensuring clients are managed in line with best practices
• demonstrate outstanding problem-solving skills, attention to detail and commitment to client satisfaction

We’re looking for a South Florida Market Administrative Officer to:

• lead the operations and administrative team, conducting performance reviews and making personnel decisions
• manage administration, including facilities, and oversee overall cost management
• make sure all employees complete required training and receive coaching on administrative policies, procedures and risk management and comply with all legal and regulatory requirements
• guarantee that the team meets service excellence standards

• act as a point of escalation for service breaks, operations issues and business continuity, resolving escalated issues from branches

jobs.title: Global Wealth Management - Investment Intern
Cards Core Processing Specialist
jobs.description:
Are you incredibly organized with an eye for detail? Do you know how to take care of business?

As a key member of the Cards Processing Team, you will be responsible for ensuring the accurate and timely settlement of daily processes to move funds between UBS and our card vendors. You’ll work cross-functionally to support operations by:

• Proven leadership experience, including coaching/mentoring peers, leading workstreams, and driving accountability for timelines and outcomes.
• Managing resolution of errors and rejections on card applications and communicating solutions and outcomes to branch staff.
• Validating card benefit and promotional requests for eligibility and processing redemptions.
• Manage with urgency the end-to-end card issuance process, including card production, personalization, and distribution.
• Reconciliation of multiple general ledgers used for card processing, researching and resolving outages.
• Accurate performance of card account maintenance requests.
• Coordinate issues to the correct system support, track for timely resolution and communicate to branch staff.
• Payment Research- to include unidentified payments, payment returns and payment reversal requests.
• Communication of errors reported by the bank to the branch staff to move the card applications forward (credit freezes, under 21 applicant requirement, additional paperwork for underwriting)

jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer, Events
jobs.description:
We are a group of professionals who enjoy identifying areas of improvement and engineering better solutions. As a crew, we do our best to create a supportive environment where each of us feel appreciated and have a chance to develop professionally.

We are looking for candidates to take the role of Infrastructure Engineer and help us to:

• Engineer, test and deploy features and releases for the IBM (International Business Machines) MQ (Message Queue) product.
• Assist in keeping the IBM MQ Messaging platform stable, performant, secure and compliant with UBS policies.
• Design, develop and deploy the next generation cloud-based ecosystem for the IBM MQ product.

The key responsibilities are:

• Develop and improve application hosting infrastructure services, with a focus on the IBM MQ product and its associated supporting infrastructure.
• Provide hosting and deployment services that are easy to consume and efficient to operate, both on-prem and in the cloud – making effective use of cloud capabilities on the Azure platform.
• Apply a broad range of engineering practices, in an agile context based on Scrum. From analyzing requirements and developing new features to automated testing, deployment, and operations. With emphasis on Gitlab, the key tooling.
• Ensure the quality, security, reliability, and compliance of solutions by applying our digital principles and implementing both functional and non-functional requirements.
• Build observability into our solutions to monitor production health, help to resolve incidents, and remediate the root cause of risks and issues.
• Learn new technologies and practices, reuse strategic platforms and standards, evaluate options, and make decisions with long-term sustainability in mind.

jobs.title: SAP PaPM Lead
jobs.description:
"The PaPM Delivery Lead supports the delivery of the Profitability and Performance Management (PaPM) capability within the ampliFi program, with accountability for ensuring that profitability, performance, and cost outcomes are delivered in line with UBS finance principles, governance, and control standards. The role is responsible for driving disciplined execution across functional design, business model validation, data readiness, testing, and production transition for PaPM scope, ensuring that performance results are transparent, explainable, and fit for management decision‑making.
The role partners closely with Finance, Data, Integration, and Technology stakeholders and acts as a functional representative for Finance and performance consumers within the delivery. It ensures that business requirements for profitability and performance measurement are translated into well‑controlled, sustainable PaPM solutions aligned with the target finance and analytics architecture.

Key Responsibilities
- Support end‑to‑end functional delivery for assigned PaPM workstreams or modules within the ampliFi finance and analytics stack, with clear ownership of outcomes.
- Contribute to and review functional designs and PaPM models to ensure alignment with agreed finance use cases, performance measurement principles, and cost and profitability logic.
- Ensure that PaPM calculations, allocations, and outputs are transparent, explainable, and aligned with Finance expectations and management reporting needs.
- Develop and maintain delivery plans covering functional scope, milestones, dependencies, and impacts across PaPM data sourcing, calculations, and downstream consumption.
- Proactively manage risks, issues, and dependencies (RAID), with a focus on calculation integrity, data quality, business interpretability, and operational readiness, escalating where required.
- Coordinate day‑to‑day delivery across Finance, Data, Integration, Testing, and Deployment teams to ensure coherent end‑to‑end execution and alignment on functional priorities.
- Ensure adherence to agreed delivery standards, finance principles, model design decisions, and change control processes across PaPM scope.
- Support SIT and UAT execution from a functional perspective, including validation of business scenarios, review of calculation results, assessment of defects based on finance impact, and support for business sign‑off.
- Assist with production readiness and cutover preparation, focusing on stability of calculations, consistency of results, reconciliation where applicable, and smooth transition to business‑as‑usual operations.
"

jobs.title: SAP FPSL& FSDM Lead
jobs.description:
"The FPSL & FSDM Delivery Lead supports the delivery of the Financial Products Subledger (FPSL) and Financial Services Data Management (FSDM) capabilities within the ampliFi program, with accountability for ensuring that finance, accounting, and data outcomes are delivered in line with UBS accounting principles, data standards, and delivery governance. The role is responsible for driving disciplined execution across functional design, business data readiness, testing, and production transition for FPSL and FSDM scope, ensuring consistent and high‑quality outcomes across the ledger and finance data landscape.
The role partners closely with Finance, Data, Integration, and Technology stakeholders and acts as a functional representative for Finance and data consumers within the delivery. It ensures that business requirements are translated into solutions that support accurate accounting, trusted finance data, and sustainable downstream reporting, while maintaining alignment with the target ledger and data architecture.

Key Responsibilities
- Support end‑to‑end functional delivery for assigned FPSL and FSDM workstreams or modules within the ampliFi ledger and finance data stack, with clear ownership of outcomes.
- Contribute to and review functional designs for FPSL and FSDM to ensure correct accounting treatment, posting logic, data definitions, and alignment with agreed ledger principles and finance data models.
- Ensure that FPSL and FSDM solutions support consistent, reconciled, and reliable finance data for downstream reporting, controls, and analytics.
- Develop and maintain delivery plans covering functional scope, milestones, dependencies, and impacts across FPSL–FSDM integration points and downstream consumers.
- Proactively manage risks, issues, and dependencies (RAID), with a focus on financial accuracy, data quality, regulatory expectations, and operational readiness, escalating where required.
- Coordinate day‑to‑day delivery across Finance, Data, Integration, Testing, and Deployment teams to ensure coherent end‑to‑end execution and alignment on functional priorities.
- Ensure adherence to agreed delivery standards, accounting principles, data standards, design decisions, and change control processes across FPSL and FSDM scope.
- Support SIT and UAT execution from a functional perspective, including validation of business scenarios, assessment of data readiness and defects based on finance impact, and support for business sign‑off.
- Assist with production readiness and cutover preparation, focusing on financial continuity, accuracy and reconciliation of balances, stability of finance data, and smooth transition to business‑as‑usual operations."

• Lead a small team in ensuring cost basis data is accurate while supporting the branch network

Research and resolve client requests for cost basis information in their accounts

• Analyze and resolve tax lot breaks through research and collaboration

• Adapt current, as well as establish new processes that allow the department to increase quality output

jobs.description:
Your role
Do you have a strategic mindset to engage with senior stakeholders? Do you have strong problem solving skills and do you enjoy bringing structure to complex situations, engage with a variety of views and stakeholders to find common solutions? Are you good at communicating complex business challenges in a constructive and easy way?

We are looking for someone who wants to:
• drive the development of the GWM strategy on a global, country, channel and product level, together with the respective businesses
• support the decision making of our internal business partners on senior management level
• conduct industry, competitor and benchmarking analyses
• design operating models and processes for specific markets and business segments
• coordinate and challenge business plans including financial modelling
• develop implementation roadmaps of strategic initiatives
• drive M&A projects from a strategic perspective (rationale, financial opportunity, due diligence, etc.)
• be open to do cross-divisional strategy projects/rotations as opportunities arise

jobs.description:
Do you have a sound knowledge of investment funds? Do you have experience in managing projects and like to engage with various tasks and people at the same time? Do you want to help us to manage our growing business within UBS Asset Management?

We’re looking for a Product Manager to:

• Coordinate client projects independently front-to-back, such as investment fund lifecycle events (e.g. new share classes, new sub-portfolio, change of asset manager) with all stakeholders and the client. This includes project planning, communication and execution
• Support complex projects, such as the set-up of a new fund or mandate, their repositioning, merger or liquidation. Coordinate and manage a project stream or defined tasks such as operational implementation, depending on the project
• Act as point of contact for day-to-day inquiries related to third party investment funds and mandates from clients and client managers, providing high quality responses and solutions, both efficiently and effectively
• Manage recurring tasks with subject matter experts and stakeholders, such as reporting, distributions, budgeting
• Data management and maintenance required to manage our fund shelf
• Assume responsibility for timely completeness of processes and documentation, consistency and accuracy of data

jobs.title: Client Onboarding Specialist (US Shift)
jobs.description:
Onboarding KYC Operations Analyst will assist with the drafting and gathering of client documents and verification of client data via public domains and internal sources at a client level prior to final review by a KYC Operations Reviewer and the client-facing team. This research is performed to ensure a client’s KYC profile is appropriately updated and any discrepancies or issues with the profile escalated to the appropriate lines of business.

• Collection and Verification of confidential client data via publicly available and internal sources.
• Understand the firm’s KYC requirements when completing documentation inclusive of Customer.
• Identification Program (CIP), Simplified Due Diligence (SDD), Basic Due Diligence (BDD), Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD).
• Take independent decisions consistent with client identification policy.
• Compare and contrast differences within KYC records, highlight and escalate any discrepancies.
• Meet firm wide quality standards.

jobs.title: Junior Initiative Manager in the Investment Bank Change Team

titleByLocale.it: Junior Initiative Manager in the Investment Bank Change Team

titleByLocale.de: Junior Initiative Manager in the Investment Bank Change Team
titleByLocale.fr: Junior Initiative Manager in the Investment Bank Change Team

jobs.description:
Are you a project manager looking for your next big challenge? Do you know what it takes to deliver complex changes in an Agile operating environment? Are you comfortable presenting to and influencing senior management stakeholders?

We're looking for an Initiative Manager to:
• manage the delivery of one (or more) new change initiative as part of larger IB-wide strategic and/or regulatory change programs. Examples of projects we handle include European T+1 Settlement, CRD VI, transaction/trade reporting regulations, and others
• manage the project plan, resources, risks, issues, delivery schedule, and quality
• steer a multidisciplinary team through the full project lifecycle
• develop strong relationships with all project stakeholders
• provide regular updates to senior management
• ensure that Run-the-Bank teams are kept informed, involved, and able to take on their new responsibilities after go-live

jobs.title: Risk Expert, IB Derivatives & Solutions
jobs.description:
Are you a sharp evaluator of risk, who enjoys investigating complex business processes and products? Do you want to play a key role in improving the effectiveness of business critical processes to help safeguard and strengthen UBS?

We’re looking for a Risk Expert or Auditor to:

• plan, execute and lead audits, issue assurance, and risk assessment reviews according to our internal standards that are fully supported by the work done.
• highlight where controls are functioning well or where UBS needs to act to better manage current and emerging risks, and escalate any significant concerns or issues appropriately.
• meet our internal standards for the quality, execution and documentation of assurance work, taking account of legal entity coverage expectations, where required.
• proactively and successfully collaborate with peers in and across audit teams.
• build and maintain constructive relationships with colleagues and relevant stakeholders at all levels including senior management.
• continuously assess the risk profile of the relevant UBS IB businesses.
• participate in regulatory inspections and address inquiries from regulators

jobs.title: CA Intern
jobs.description:
Interested in working in finance, and specifically want to help produce daily and monthly P&L statements as well as balance sheets, but not sure where to start? Do you want to work for a firm with a truly global footprint that consistently ranks among the world's top financial institutions? We're looking for ambitious students to join our 2025 12-month internship program within Group Finance.
You’ll get to: 
• have a better understanding of P&L and balance sheet
• compile and analyze financial information, help close books at month-end, clear breaks, post journals and learn how to explain significant P&L changes
• build relationships with internal and external clients
• understand our industry, culture and strategy

jobs.title: Premises Assistant Manager
jobs.description:
The Premises Assistant Manager supports the effective delivery of day‑to‑day facilities operations by working closely with third‑party service providers to ensure a safe, efficient, compliant and engaging workplace. The role has a strong operational focus across staff restaurants, transportation services, hospitality, soft services, and general facilities management, while ensuring high service standards, cost control, and a positive employee experience.

Key 
• Responsibilities
Facilities Operations & Service Delivery

Support the day‑to‑day management of facilities operations, ensuring seamless delivery of services across the premises, including staff restaurants, transportation, housekeeping, mailroom, security coordination, and other workplace services

Act as the operational interface with third‑party suppliers, ensuring service delivery aligns with agreed contracts, KPIs, and UBS standards.

Monitor supplier performance, conduct regular service reviews, and proactively address service gaps or operational risks

Vendor & Contract Management

Support contract administration and governance for facilities-related vendors, including catering and transport partners.

Track KPIs, SLAs, and compliance requirements, escalating issues where service delivery or safety standards are at risk.

Assist with cost monitoring, invoice validation, and identification of efficiency or optimization opportunities

Workplace Experience & Employee Engagement

Ensure staff restaurants and transport services deliver a consistently positive employee experience, balancing quality, safety, and cost efficiency.

Support workplace initiatives aimed at improving employee satisfaction, engagement, and well‑being.

Health, Safety & Compliance

Support compliance with local statutory requirements, building safety norms, fire safety, and internal UBS policies.

Promote a strong safety culture across all facilities services and suppliers, ensuring risks are identified and mitigated proactively

The role has a strong operational focus across staff restaurants, transportation services, hospitality, soft services, and general facilities management, while ensuring high service standards, cost control, and a positive employee experience. Key
• Responsibilities Facilities Operations & Service Delivery Support the day‑to‑day management of facilities operations, ensuring seamless delivery of services across the premises, including staff restaurants, transportation, housekeeping, mailroom, security coordination, and other workplace services Act as the operational interface with third‑party suppliers, ensuring service delivery aligns with agreed contracts, KPIs, and UBS standards. Monitor supplier performance, conduct regular service reviews, and proactively address service gaps or operational risks Vendor & Contract Management Support contract administration and governance for facilities-related vendors, including catering and transport partners. Track KPIs, SLAs, and compliance requirements, escalating issues where service delivery or safety standards are at risk. Assist with cost monitoring, invoice validation, and identification of efficiency or optimization opportunities Workplace Experience & Employee Engagement Ensure staff restaurants and transport services deliver a consistently positive employee experience, balancing quality, safety, and cost efficiency. Support workplace initiatives aimed at improving employee satisfaction, engagement, and well‑being. Health, Safety & Compliance Support compliance with local statutory requirements, building safety norms, fire safety, and internal UBS policies. Promote a strong safety culture across all facilities services and suppliers, ensuring risks are identified and mitigated proactively

We’re looking for someone like that to support UBSS Entity COO and:
• oversee and manage the entire daily operation of the entity
• improve operational efficiency and reduce operational costs

jobs.title: Spezialist/in Hypotheken und Grundbuchwesen 80 – 100%
jobs.description:
Hast du Erfahrungen in der Hypothekarverarbeitung und dem Schweizerischen Grundbuchwesen?
Ist effizientes, exaktes und verantwortungsbewusstes Arbeiten genau deine Stärke?
Hast du Freude an administrativen Tätigkeiten mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten?

Dann bewirb dich, denn wir wollen wachsen und suchen Verstärkung!

Deine Aufgaben:
• Selbständige Abwicklung der bewilligten Kreditgesuche
• Erstellen von allen notwendigen Kredit- und Sicherungsdokumenten
• Abbilden und Bewirtschaften von Hypotheken und Sicherheiten (z.B. Grundpfänder, Policen, Bürgschaften)
• Erstellen von Zahlungsversprechen sowie fristgerechter Ausführung von Zahlungen
• Sicherstellung aller Bedingungen für die Freigabe der Hypotheken und Zahlungen
• Begleitung und korrekte Terminierung der Geschäftsfälle in Eigenverantwortung
• Korrespondenz mit Grundbuchämtern, Notaren, Banken und Versicherungsgesellschaften
• Pflichtbewusster Umgang mit potenziellen Risiken

jobs.title: Conseiller-ère Clientèle privée Affluent - Sion (80-100%)
jobs.description:
Avez-vous une personnalité sociable qui prend des initiatives ? Conseiller les clients sur les nouvelles technologies vous motive ?

Afin de soutenir notre croissance, nous recherchons justement une collègue ou un collègue qui a du flair pour les attentes des clients et qui se sent bien dans le monde digital.

Et voici vos tâches principales :
• vous identifiez précisément les besoins des clients et les conseillez de manière compétente
• vous avez un sens aigu des solutions optimales. Avec ça vous garantissez la relation clientèle sur le long terme et marquez des points auprès de nouveaux clients
• dans votre conseil, vous misez sur des Tools et des Apps innovants. De ce fait, vous emmenez notre clientèle dans notre voyage vers le futur: vous vous encouragez à utiliser nos produits digitaux et vous vous y investissez

jobs.title: Client Relationship Manager, ETF & Index Fund

titleByLocale.it: Client Relationship Manager, ETF & Index Fund
titleByLocale.en: Client Relationship Manager, ETF & Index Fund
titleByLocale.de: Client Relationship Manager, ETF & Index Fund
titleByLocale.fr: Client Relationship Manager, ETF & Index Fund

jobs.description:
Do you have a track record of successfully managing client relationships? Are you able to create ‎collaborative solutions in challenging and complex situations?

If so, we are looking for a Client Coverage person for UBS ETF & Index Fund in Singapore:

• cover the strategic development and management of the ETF & Index Fund business Singapore and SEA region. Positioned within one of the key growth areas of the company, you will play a critical role in driving the growth and success of their ETF offerings, overseeing all relevant aspects of product development, distribution, marketing, and operations. This position requires a combination of strategic vision, market insight, collaborative mindset, strong leadership abilities and out-of-the-box thinking to position the UBS ETF & Index Fund business for long-term success in a competitive landscape.
• involved in development and execution of a comprehensive strategy for the UBS ETF & Index Fund business for Singapore, aligned with the overall goals and objectives of the firm.
• expand distribution channels and increase market penetration for UBS ETFs & Index Funds in Singapore through effective marketing initiatives, digital engagement, partnerships, and client interaction to increase sales growth, generate revenues and UBS’s position in the market.
• build and maintain strong relationships with wealth management, institutional and wholesale clients, financial advisors, and other key stakeholders to understand their needs and preferences, and to effectively position UBS ETFs & Index Funds.
• collaborate with internal colleagues regionally and globally including portfolio management, capital markets, marketing and product development and support the efforts of the generalist wholesale and institutional client coverage teams. identify opportunities for new UBS ETFs & Index Funds and develop enhancements to existing offerings, working closely with the investment, research, and product development teams.
• ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements related to UBS ETFs & Index Funds, staying abreast of industry developments and implementing necessary changes to maintain compliance.
• monitor and analyse the investment performance and sales results of UBS ETF & Index Fund products, tracking key metrics and performance drivers to inform decision-making and drive continuous improvement. Represent the firm in industry forums, conferences, and other events to enhance UBS’s visibility and reputation within the indexing marketplace.

jobs.title: Trade Support Specialist
jobs.description:
Are you ready to make an impact in Asset Management Japan?

We are looking for Trade Support Specialist to:
•Process transactions and release settlement instructions for ITM funds and mandate accounts across multiple asset classes, including equities, fixed income, FX, derivatives, and onshore/offshore funds
• •Ensure the accuracy and completeness of trade data for NAV calculation, performance measurement, client reporting, and coordinate with trust banks and internal teams involved in NAV calculation.
• •Perform daily operational tasks using core accounting systems such as T-Star/TX and FAIMS, and support the setup and launch of ITM funds and mandate accounts
• •Support internal control requirements and audit-related activities and documentation, and adapt to new products and investment structures by applying established operational processes

jobs.title: Senior Client Reporting Specialist
jobs.description:
Are you interested in working at Fund Reporting Team in the Asset Management?

We are looking for a Senior Fund Reporting Specialist to:

jobs.title: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyst

titleByLocale.it: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analista
titleByLocale.en: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.de: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.fr: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyste

jobs.description:
We are looking for a Market risk quant in the Market risk model validation team in India to perform:
• Independent validation of market risk models in line with the UBS model governance policy and regulatory requirements. Key responsibilities include
• Assess the model's conceptual soundness and methodology
• Check the appropriateness/quality of input data used for model development, review appropriateness of model parameters, accuracy of the model calibration etc.
• Review model outcome, impacts, or develop alternate benchmark approaches to challenge model outputs (as appropriate)
• Assess model risk, providing effective challenge to model assumptions and evaluate model limitations
• Write comprehensive validation reports, documenting the assessments to required standards
• Collaborate effectively with stakeholders globally to understand / challenge potential model risks; communicate validation findings to relevant stakeholders
• Support ongoing review of model performance and model change reviews to ensure models remain fit for purpose

jobs.title: Internship in Corporate Action Team
jobs.description:
Do you have a sharp eye for detail?
Are you known for hitting deadlines?
We’re looking for someone like that to help us in:
• assisting in monitoring and processing corporate actions (e.g., mergers, dividends, spin-offs, etc.),
• supporting the analysis and classification of events received from data vendors, custodians, and other sources,
• helping ensure timely and accurate notification of events to internal stakeholders and clients,
• participating in process documentation, exception handling, and control checks,
• collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure data integrity and service quality.

jobs.title: Investment Banker - Equity Capital Markets (ECM)
jobs.description:
Are you a leader in innovation? Do you know how to come up with creative ideas for clients?

We’re looking for an investment banker to:

• create partnerships by increasing depth and breadth of existing client relationships
• increase the returns of products and services
• generate new ideas and creative approaches that push the business forward
• assist in planning overall resource requirements, by recruiting, developing and retaining talented individuals
• make sound and balanced judgments (and always within professional, ethical, regulatory and legal standards)
• motivate and inspire others by providing a vision of shared goals

Detailed salary information :
• New York: the salary range for this role is $275000 to $275000
The expected salary range(s) for this role as of the date of this posting is/are based on factors including, but not limited to, experience, qualifications, education, location and skill level. This role may also be eligible for discretionary incentive compensation. For benefits information, please visit ubs.com/usbenefits .

jobs.title: Client Account Manager
jobs.description:
Do you want to proactively go the extra mile with us to help clients? Are you focused on the details, even when under pressure?

We’re looking for a Client Account Manager (CAM) to:
• support our private bankers known as Client Advisors in our Southeast Asia desks in Singapore to deliver exceptional client service and experience (our assistant private bankers are called CAMs)
• be point-of-entry contact for clients with account queries or orders, stock orders, payments, etc.
• assist with account opening, onboarding, due diligence and monitoring activities
• support with documentation for client meetings, investment proposals and the like
• collaborate with colleagues to ensure that all client accounts comply with legal, compliance and regulatory requirements
